human_verification.notes: Reviewed the refreshed package for AWS-architecture specificity, source quality, boundary control, scenario coverage, functionality coverage, research completeness, deployment notes, commercialization notes, and specification-based runtime posture.

functional_audit.notes: All required shared, general workflow-phase, and cross-industry artifact-awareness abilities are mapped to passing scenarios.

required_source_classes_covered: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
required_scenario_families_covered: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

deployment_readiness.notes: Deployment documentation defines spec-pack artifact loading, tenant context prerequisites, boundary enforcement, rollback, monitoring, and runtime materializer requirements.

promotion_criteria: repeated invocation volume proves the lane is called often enough to justify retaining a generated implementation
generated implementation passes the same 18-scenario suite with no unreviewed regressions
source refresh and tenant evidence handling remain current
repository maintainers approve trusted-build retention separately
runtime_promotion.notes: This validated package is canonical as a spec pack and does not claim retained implementation ownership.

evidence_artifacts.purpose: canonical spec-first manifest with boundary, sources, workflow, materialization, validation, and validated metadata
evaluation pass evidence and functionality coverage summary
deployment, rollback, tenant isolation, materialization, monitoring, and promotion notes
market positioning, packaging, subscription, proof points, and go-to-market gaps

notes: Structured evidence supports the validated claim required by issue #1633.
